heading: Comparability in compensation schedules
label: 1833b Comparability in compensation schedules
notes: CodificationSection was enacted as part of the Financial Institutions Reform, Recovery, and Enforcement Act of 1989, and not as part of the Federal Deposit Insurance Act which comprises this chapter.

children.heading: In general
children.label: a In general
children.text: The Federal Deposit Insurance Corporation, the Comptroller of the Currency, the National Credit Union Administration Board, the Federal Housing Finance Agency, the Office of Financial Research, and the Bureau of Consumer Financial Protection, the Farm Credit Administration, in establishing and adjusting schedules of compensation and benefits which are to be determined solely by each agency under applicable provisions of law, shall inform the heads of the other agencies and the Congress of such compensation and benefits and shall seek to maintain comparability regarding compensation and benefits.1So in original. Probably should be “Research, the Bureau of Consumer Financial Protection, and the”.1
inform the heads of the agencies referred to in subsection (a) and Congress of such compensation and benefits; and
seek to maintain comparability with those agencies regarding compensation and benefits.
